title: Getting started with admixr2
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models summary-level data. clinical study supply: E — observed mean vector (one entry per observation time) V — observed covariance matrix (variance vector) n — sample size times — observation time points ev — dosing event table estimators match E V model-predicted counterparts return standard nlmixr2 fit object. lets apply established nlmixr2 models aggregate statistics publications internal data summaries individual records unavailable. Three estimators available: adfo natural starting point model screening initial estimates. admc workhorse standard PK models. adirmc preferred complex ODE systems expensive solves, high-dimensional IIV, poor starting values. See vignette("estimator-comparison", package = "admixr2") detailed comparison.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: examplomycin ships admixr2: 500 simulated subjects two-compartment PK model first-order absorption (100 mg oral dose, sampled 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12 h). True parameters: CL = 5 L/h, V1 = 10 L, V2 = 30 L, Q = 10 L/h, ka = 1 h⁻¹; IIV = 0.3 (SD log scale) parameters; proportional error SD = 0.2.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Reshape individual records subjects × times matrix, compute E V: V 9×9 sample covariance matrix. -diagonal entries capture within-subject correlation across time; using full matrix (method = "cov") typically tightens parameter estimates compared diagonal-approximation (method = "var"). admixr2 auto-detects method structure V.
code: obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)                     # 500  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  round(E, 2) #> [1] 0.97 1.94 2.79 3.02 2.26 1.65 1.06 0.75 0.51

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Models use standard nlmixr2 syntax mu-referenced log-scale parameters: Writing parameter exp(tcl + eta.cl) called mu-referencing: structural fixed effect random effect enter additively log scale. admixr2 exploits pairing compute analytical gradients via sensitivity equations. See Advanced usage vignette details, including parameters without random effect handled.
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Bundle study’s statistics named list:
code: study <- list(   E     = E,   V     = V,                       # full 9x9 covariance matrix   n     = n,   times = times,   ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100)    # single 100 mg oral dose )

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Pass one named studies admControl():
code: fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies   = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim     = 5000L,     cov_n_sim = 10000L,     maxeval   = 300L,     seed      = 1L   ) ) #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:07

title: Advanced usage
text: study’s V determines branch NLL used. admixr2 auto-detects method: Override setting method explicitly study list: Use "cov" full covariance matrix expect temporal correlation inform parameter estimates. Use "var" marginal variances available (e.g. published table means SDs) runtime priority.
code: # Force full covariance NLL even for a diagonal V study_cov <- c(study, list(method = "cov"))  # Diagonal approximation when you only have marginal variances study_var <- list(   E      = E,   V      = diag(diag(V)),   # drop off-diagonal entries   n      = n,   times  = times,   ev     = rxode2::et(amt = 100),   method = "var" )

title: Advanced usage
text: admControl() adfoControl() offer gradient strategies via grad argument. adirmcControl() offers "analytical", "none", "fd" ("sens" "cfd"). admControl() gradient modes: adfoControl() gradient modes: adfo, gradient modes still use sensitivity model inside NLL evaluation compute J single rxSolve — grad controls optimizer gradient formed. "sens" recommended standard ODE linCmt() models. sensitivity model unavailable (e.g. rare ODE features), admixr2 falls back "fd" automatically warning.
code: # Analytical gradient (default; fastest for ODE models) fit_sens <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "sens",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))  # Derivative-free — ignores maxeval; use nloptr stopping criteria instead fit_bobyqa <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "none",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))

title: Advanced usage
text: Mu-referencing nlmixr2 convention expressing structural parameter fixed effect plus random effect known back-transformation: admixr2 uses pairing classify parameters three cases, handled differently grad = "sens": Paired (mu-referenced) parameters — rxode2 augments ODE system sensitivity equations d(pred)/d(eta_i) random effect. tcl eta.cl enter additively log scale, gradient NLL respect tcl equals gradient respect eta.cl. recovered single ODE solve, extra function evaluations. Non-mu-referenced parameters — structural parameter random effect written separately (e.g. cl <- exp(tcl) * exp(eta.cl)), admixr2 recover d(NLL)/d(tcl) sensitivity equations alone. sensitivity model still used etas, omega, sigma; unpaired structural parameters require targeted CRN finite difference solve. warning issued grad = "sens" kept. Parameters without random effect — structural parameters corresponding eta (e.g. v2 <- exp(tv2) IIV dropped V2) always handled CRN finite differences. perturbation uses quasi-random seed nominal draw Monte Carlo noise largely cancels difference.
code: cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)   # mu-referenced: tcl paired with eta.cl

title: Advanced usage
text: IRMC estimator draws proposals inflated omega. nonlinear models, mean simulated predictions equal prediction eta = 0 — Jensen’s inequality gap. kappa correction shifts predicted mean account bias: "linear" (default): precomputes J = df/d(theta) per outer iteration via small FD batch. kappa_fn(theta_cand) ≈ f0 + J %*% (theta_cand - theta0) — pure arithmetic per inner step, zero extra rxSolve calls inner optimisation. "first-order": re-evaluates f(theta_cand, 0) via rxSolve inner step. exact one rxSolve per inner NLL evaluation.
code: adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "first-order")   # default adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "linear")

title: Advanced usage
text: Multi-restart fitting guards local optima. workers > 1 runs restarts parallel; admixr2 manages worker pool internally: cores distributed automatically: floor(total_cores / n_workers) threads per worker, remainder allocated cyclically (e.g. 13 cores / 4 workers → 4, 3, 3, 3). Workers stopped restart phase cores freed covariance Hessian step. Call admStopWorkers() manually fit interrupted cleanup.
code: fit_par <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies    = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim      = 5000L,     n_restarts = 4L,     workers    = 4L,      # fork on Unix/macOS; PSOCK cluster on Windows     cores      = 8L,      # total rxSolve OpenMP threads across workers     restart_sd = 0.3,     # SD of log-scale perturbation from the starting point     seed       = 1L   ) )

title: Advanced usage
text: sampling argument controls eta samples drawn: Sobol sequences typically require 2–5× fewer samples plain normal draws equivalent NLL variance. small n_sim, "lhs" provides better coverage Sobol.

title: Advanced usage
text: covMethod = "r" (default) computes numerical Hessian optimisation reports standard errors print(fit). larger cov_n_sim reduces MC noise Hessian: Set covMethod = "none" skip uncertainty estimation early model development need point estimates: Hessian non-positive-definite (SEs printed NA), increase cov_h_outer cov_n_sim.
code: fit_cov <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies     = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim       = 5000L,     cov_n_sim   = 10000L,   # more samples → lower Hessian noise     cov_h_outer = 2.5e-3,   # outer FD step scale (default: eps^(1/5) ≈ 2.5e-3)     covMethod   = "r",     seed        = 1L   ) ) admControl(..., covMethod = "none")

title: Advanced usage
text: Standard information criteria work directly admFit objects. AIC BIC comparable within estimator — adfo evaluates linearised likelihood differs MC likelihood used admc adirmc, cross-estimator comparisons meaningful. compare full model (IIV five parameters) reduced model IIV CL V1 : Lower AIC/BIC favours parsimonious model; difference > 10 generally considered strong evidence.

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "mean" panel 2×2 grid per study. Requires patchwork composite layout; without , four separate plots returned. Mean diagnostics examplomycin study. Top-left — Observed: sample mean ±1 SD ribbon, SD = √diag(V_obs). Top-right — Predicted: predicted mean ±1 SD ribbon, SD = √diag(V_pred). V_pred combines -subject variability (Omega) residual error (sigma). top panels share y-axis scale differences magnitude immediately visible. Bottom-left — Raw residual: E_obs[t] − μ_pred[t] lollipop. grey band ±2 SE(mean), SE = √(V_pred[t,t] / n). Points outside band indicate systematic bias time point. Bottom-right — Standardised residual: z[t] = residual[t] / SE[t]. well-specified model, z approximately N(0, 1). Stars flag |z| > 1.96 (∗), 2.58 (∗∗), 3.29 (∗∗∗). 9 time points multiplicity correction, one flagged time point expected chance alone.
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "mean")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "cov" panel compares observed predicted (co)variance heatmaps. Requires patchwork composite layout. Covariance diagnostics: observed predicted covariance matrices residuals. Top row — Observed | Predicted: matrices plotted shared blue-white-red scale. good fit shows matching patterns magnitude sign — including -diagonal temporal correlation structure. Bottom-left — Residual (V_obs − V_pred): diverging scale (purple-white-teal). Persistent positive residuals diagonal mean model -predicts variance; negative residuals indicate -prediction. Bottom-right — Standardised residual: entry divided asymptotic SE. Diagonal SE = √(2 V[,]² / (n−1)); -diagonal SE = √((V[,]·V[j,j] + V[,j]²) / (n−1)). Stars use thresholds mean panel.
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "cov")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "nll" panel shows objective function evolved across optimizer iterations, coloured restart: NLL convergence trace. line one optimizer restart. Restarts converge final value support unimodal landscape. spread final NLL values suggests local optima — increase n_restarts restart_sd admControl().
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "nll")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "par" panel facets parameter’s trajectory optimizer iterations: Parameter trace natural scale. Struct thetas back-transformed; sigma shown SD; V(eta) = variance. Parameters displayed natural scale: Structural thetas: back-transformed (e.g. exp() log-scale params) Sigma: displayed SD Omega diagonal: displayed variance, labelled V(eta.x) Omega -diagonal: raw Cholesky L[,j] Smoothly converging traces stabilise well maxeval indicate optimizer artificially cut . traces still drift end, increase maxeval.

title: Diagnostic plots
text: estimated Omega matrix can visualised correlation heatmap inspect inter-individual variability structure. especially informative models -diagonal omega entries. IIV correlation matrix derived estimated Omega.
code: omega   <- fit$env$admExtra$omega eta_nms <- fit$env$admExtra$eta_col_names if (is.null(eta_nms)) eta_nms <- paste0("eta.", seq_len(nrow(omega)))  corr_mat <- cov2cor(omega) rownames(corr_mat) <- colnames(corr_mat) <- eta_nms  df_corr <- expand.grid(   eta_i = factor(eta_nms, levels = rev(eta_nms)),   eta_j = factor(eta_nms, levels = eta_nms),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df_corr$r <- as.vector(t(corr_mat))  ggplot2::ggplot(df_corr, ggplot2::aes(x = eta_j, y = eta_i, fill = r)) +   ggplot2::geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = round(r, 2)), size = 3.2) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(     low = "#2166AC", mid = "white", high = "#D6604D",     midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "r"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title    = "IIV correlation (estimated Omega)",     subtitle = "Off-diagonal entries are zero for a diagonal Omega model",     x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::theme_bw() +   ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: three estimators accept model study specification return admFit object. share common likelihood framework differ approximate population mean covariance predicted observations.

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: study observed data mean vector y‾\bar{y} covariance matrix SS computed nn subjects. multivariate normal approximation aggregate log-likelihood −2ℓ=n[log|Vpred|+tr(Vpred−1S)+r⊤Vpred−1r],r=y‾−μpred -2\ell = n \left[   \log |V_\text{pred}| +   \operatorname{tr}(V_\text{pred}^{-1} S) +   r^\top V_\text{pred}^{-1} r \right], \qquad r = \bar{y} - \mu_\text{pred} μpred\mu_\text{pred} VpredV_\text{pred} model-predicted population mean covariance observations. nonlinear model f(θ,η)f(\theta,\eta) integrals random-effects distribution η∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega): μpred=𝔼η[f(θ,η)],Vpred=Var⁡η[f(θ,η)]+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \mathbb{E}_\eta[f(\theta, \eta)], \qquad V_\text{pred} = \operatorname{Var}_\eta[f(\theta, \eta)] + \Sigma integrals closed form nonlinear ff. three estimators minimise objective take different approaches evaluating .

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: adfo sidesteps integral entirely approximating f(θ,η)f(\theta, \eta) first-order Taylor expansion around η=0\eta = 0: f(θ,η)≈f(θ,0)+Jη,Jt,j=∂ft∂ηj|η=0 f(\theta, \eta) \approx f(\theta, 0) + J\,\eta, \qquad J_{t,j} = \left.\frac{\partial f_t}{\partial \eta_j}\right|_{\eta=0} Substituting population moments gives closed-form expressions: μpred=f(θ,0),Vpred=JΩJ⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = f(\theta, 0), \qquad V_\text{pred} = J\,\Omega\,J^\top + \Sigma Jacobian JJ obtained single rxSolve call via sensitivity equations augment ODE system ∂f/∂ηj\partial f / \partial \eta_j — finite differences, extra solves per eta. makes adfo fastest estimator: one rxSolve per NLL evaluation, fully deterministic. approximation holds: Taylor expansion exact ff linear η\eta. nonlinear models large IIV true population covariance exceeds JΩJ⊤J\Omega J^\top; FO underestimates Ω\Omega bias grows degree nonlinearity. AIC note: adfo evaluates linearised likelihood. objective scale admc adirmc must used cross-estimator AIC comparisons.

what: Monte Carlo (MC): sample average over η\eta
title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: admc estimates population integrals directly drawing NN samples ηi∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega) computing sample moments: μpred=1N∑=1Nf(θ,ηi),Vpred=1N∑=1N(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{=1}^N f(\theta, \eta_i), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{=1}^N   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)^\top + \Sigma approximation made ff — estimator asymptotically exact N→∞N \\infty. Sobol quasi-random sequences (default) reduce MC variance relative plain normal draws, typically requiring 2–5× fewer samples equivalent precision. key cost every NLL evaluation requires NN rxSolve calls — one per sample. analytical gradient (sensitivity equations mu-referenced parameters; common random numbers FD otherwise) adds solves, base cost per optimizer step remains N×N \times (cost one rxSolve). Advantages: Asymptotically exact; AIC directly comparable across admc fits. Works well standard 1–2 compartment PK models moderate IIV. Limitations: NLL evaluation requires NN rxSolve calls; slower adfo. MC noise gradient can cause optimiser oscillation low n_sim.

what: Iterative Reweighting MC (IRMC): proposals fixed, inner loop free
title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: adirmc addresses main bottleneck admc — need NN new rxSolve calls every optimizer step — decoupling proposal generation optimization. outer phase, NN proposals ηi\eta_i drawn held fixed entire inner optimization. Given fixed proposals, evaluating NLL requires matrix operations — new rxSolve calls. proposals reweighted likelihood current Ω\Omega inner objective remains valid approximation parameters move: wi∝p(ηi∣Ω)q(ηi),μpred=∑iwif(θ,ηi),Vpred=∑iwi(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)(⋯)⊤+Σ w_i \propto \frac{p(\eta_i \mid \Omega)}{q(\eta_i)}, \qquad \mu_\text{pred} = \sum_i w_i\, f(\theta, \eta_i), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \sum_i w_i\,   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(\cdots\bigr)^\top + \Sigma inner optimizer therefore runs convergence negligible cost; proposals refreshed phases. Box constraints parameters progressively tightened across phases guide global convergence. decoupling makes adirmc particularly well-suited complex ODE models rxSolve expensive: total number solves scales number phases rather number optimizer steps. also robust poor starting values inner optimisation deterministic depend re-sampling. Advantages: Far fewer rxSolve calls per optimizer step admc — especially beneficial complex ODE systems expensive solves. Robust poor starting values; deterministic inner loop. AIC directly comparable admc. Limitations: Heavier per-phase overhead admc simple, well-initialised problems cheap ODE solves.

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: model uses mu-referenced parameterisation (cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)), enables analytical gradient computation via sensitivity equations three estimators. See Advanced usage vignette details.
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  study <- list(E = E, V = V, n = n, times = times, ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100))

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
text: adfo admc recover values close true parameters (CL = 5, V1 = 10, V2 = 30, Q = 10, ka = 1; IIV variance = 0.09 ; prop.sd = 0.2). examplomycin dataset IIV moderate model near-linear η\eta, FO bias small. Parameter estimates vs true values

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc and adirmc
code: # adfo key arguments (fastest; linearised likelihood) adfoControl(   studies    = list(...),   grad       = "none",      # "none" (BOBYQA), "analytical", "fd", "cfd"   maxeval    = 500L,   n_restarts = 1L,   covMethod  = "r",         # "r" = numerical Hessian, "none" = skip   seed       = 1L )  # admc key arguments admControl(   studies    = list(...),   n_sim      = 5000L,       # MC sample count   grad       = "sens",      # gradient mode: "sens", "fd", "cfd", "none"   n_restarts = 1L,          # number of optimizer restarts   workers    = 1L,          # parallel workers for restarts   covMethod  = "r",         # "r" = numerical Hessian, "none" = skip   seed       = 1L )  # adirmc key arguments adirmcControl(   studies         = list(...),   n_sim           = 5000L,   phases          = c(2, 1, 0.5, 0.1),   # box constraint half-widths per phase   omega_expansion = 1.5,                   # inflate proposal Omega   grad            = "analytical",          # "analytical", "none", "fd"   kappa_method    = "first-order",         # "first-order" (default) or "linear"   seed            = 1L )

title: Multiple studies
text: Passing several studies admControl() fits model simultaneously, minimising sum per-study NLLs shared set population parameters. core use case aggregate-data modelling: summary statistics multiple trials (may differ dose, sample size, observation schedule) want single population model consistent .

title: Multiple studies
text: partition 500 examplomycin subjects two cohorts 250 compute separate aggregate statistics :
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  # Alternate subjects into two equal cohorts idx1 <- seq(1, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 1, 3, 5, ... → cohort 1 idx2 <- seq(2, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 2, 4, 6, ... → cohort 2  E1 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx1, ]); V1 <- cov.wt(dv_mat[idx1, ], method = "ML")$cov; n1 <- length(idx1) E2 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx2, ]); V2 <- cov.wt(dv_mat[idx2, ], method = "ML")$cov; n2 <- length(idx2)

title: Multiple studies
text: fitting, visualise raw summary statistics confirm two cohorts comparable (drawn population ): Observed mean ± 1 SD cohort log time axis.
code: df_obs <- rbind(   data.frame(cohort = "Cohort 1", time = times,              mean = E1,              lo   = E1 - sqrt(diag(V1)),              hi   = E1 + sqrt(diag(V1))),   data.frame(cohort = "Cohort 2", time = times,              mean = E2,              lo   = E2 - sqrt(diag(V2)),              hi   = E2 + sqrt(diag(V2))) )  ggplot(df_obs, aes(x = time, y = mean, colour = cohort, fill = cohort)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_x_log10(breaks = times, labels = times) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Cohort 1" = "#0072B2", "Cohort 2" = "#D55E00")) +   scale_fill_manual(  values = c("Cohort 1" = "#0072B2", "Cohort 2" = "#D55E00")) +   labs(title    = "Observed mean ± 1 SD by cohort",        x        = "Time (h, log scale)",        y        = "Concentration",        colour   = NULL, fill = NULL) +   theme_bw()

title: Multiple studies
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models directly aggregate-level data — observed mean vector E covariance matrix V reported per clinical study — rather requiring individual patient records. integrates nlmixr2 / rxode2 ecosystem provides three estimation backends:

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: Model-Based Meta-Analysis (MBMA) pharmacometric framework synthesising evidence across multiple clinical studies fitting shared mechanistic PK/PD model aggregate outcomes (means, variances) reported study. Unlike classical meta-analysis, pools effect estimates, MBMA preserves full pharmacometric model structure — including nonlinear dose-response, inter-individual variability, residual error — enabling principled extrapolation dose optimisation across evidence base. admixr2 fits single population model jointly studies. -study differences outcomes accounted population model structure: inter-individual variability captures subject-level spread within study, residual error absorbs remaining discrepancies. study contributes dosing regimen, observation times, sample size, shares structural variance parameters.

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: Individual patient data unavailable — common scenario MBMA. Published papers report means standard deviations; regulatory submissions competitive reasons prevent individual patient data sharing across companies institutions. admixr2 extracts maximum information publicly available. Leveraging literature trial design — fit mechanistic PK/PD model aggregated results existing trials, simulate new dosing regimens patient populations committing costly study. Combining evidence across heterogeneous trials — studies differ dose, formulation, population, observation schedule. admixr2 handles multi-study fits per-study dosing events time grids single shared population model. Reproducing extending published models — digitised mean concentration– time profiles figures sufficient input. individual patient data required.

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2)  # 1. Compute aggregate statistics from individual data (or digitise from paper) data("examplomycin") obs    <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs    <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times  <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids    <- unique(obs$ID) dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  # 2. Define the model (standard nlmixr2 syntax) pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5);  label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10); label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30); label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10); label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1);  label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v1 ~ 0.09; eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09; eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2); q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  # 3. Fit fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies = list(examplomycin = list(       E = E, V = V, n = length(ids),       times = times, ev = et(amt = 100)     )),     n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L   ) )  print(fit) plot(fit)

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
text: Creates control object nlmixr2(est = "adfo").  FO estimator linearises model predictions \(\eta = 0\): faster MC estimator less accurate models large IIV strongly non-linear individual predictions.

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
code: adfoControl(   studies = list(),   grad = c("none", "analytical", "fd", "cfd"),   algorithm = "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA",   maxeval = 500L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps^(1/2),   print = 10L,   seed = 12345L,   cores = 1L,   grad_h = 1e-04,   grad_bounds = 5,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/5),   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.5,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
text: studies Named list study specifications (format admControl(): E, V, n, times, ev, optional method). grad Gradient mode.  "none" (default) uses derivative-free BOBYQA; "analytical" uses closed-form FO gradient (requires sensitivity equations); "fd" uses forward finite differences full NLL; "cfd" uses central finite differences struct theta gradient (accurate "fd", roughly twice many NLL evaluations per step). algorithm nloptr algorithm.  Automatically coerced "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" grad != "none". maxeval Maximum function evaluations (default 500). ftol_rel Relative tolerance (default sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)). print Print-frequency live progress (0 = silent). seed Random seed (used restarts). cores OpenMP threads rxSolve() (default 1). grad_h Finite-difference step unpaired struct theta gradient FD Jacobian. grad_bounds Box-constraint half-width using gradients. cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). Default 1e-3. cov_h_outer Outer step scale NLL-FD Hessian. covMethod "r" computes covariance via numerical Hessian; "none" skips . n_restarts Number optimizer restarts (1 = multi-start). restart_sd Standard deviation random perturbations initial struct thetas restart (> 1). workers Number parallel PSOCK/fork workers multi-restart (default 1 = sequential). rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation. returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object. ... Unused arguments (trigger error).

code: adirmcControl(   studies = list(),   n_sim = 2500L,   outer_iter = 50L,   sampling = c("sobol", "halton", "torus", "lhs", "rnorm"),   algorithm = "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA",   maxeval = 5000L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps,   print = 1L,   omega_expansion = 1,   seed = 12345L,   cores = 1L,   grad = c("analytical", "none", "fd"),   kappa_method = c("first-order", "linear"),   grad_h = 1e-04,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/5),   phases = c(2, 1, 0.5, 0.01),   convcrit = 0.05,   max_worse = 3L,   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   cov_n_sim = 10000L,   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.2,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
text: studies Named list study specifications. element list : E – observed mean vector V – observed covariance matrix variance vector (auto-detected) n – sample size times – numeric vector observation times ev – rxode2::et() dosing event table method – "cov" "var" (optional; auto-detected V) n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples per NLL evaluation. outer_iter Maximum inner optimiser iterations per phase. sampling Sampling method eta draws: "sobol" (Sobol, default), "halton" (Halton), "torus" (Kronecker/torus), "lhs" (Latin hypercube), "rnorm" (iid normal). algorithm nloptr algorithm string. Automatically switched "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" grad != "none". maxeval Maximum number optimizer function evaluations. ftol_rel Relative function-value tolerance convergence. print Print progress every many evaluations (0 = silent). omega_expansion Inflate proposal Omega factor (>= 1). seed Random seed reproducibility. cores Number OpenMP threads rxSolve(). grad Gradient mode inner optimiser: "analytical" (default, closed-form weight-path gradient), "none" (derivative-free BOBYQA), "fd" (finite differences). Note: "sens" "cfd" available IRMC estimator. kappa_method Kappa correction method models non-mu-referenced struct thetas: "first-order" (default, population prediction f(theta, 0), one rxSolve per inner step) "linear" (first-order Taylor expansion — precomputes J = df/d(theta) per outer iteration, zero rxSolve per inner step). grad_h Step size finite-difference gradient evaluation optimization (used grad = "fd" "cfd"). default 1e-4 near optimal balance truncation error (grows h) MC noise amplification (grows 1/h) forward FD. Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. Increase 1e-2 Hessian non-positive definite. cov_h_outer Outer step scale numerical Hessian. actual step parameter p max(|p|, 0.1) * cov_h_outer. Applied gradient-FD Hessian (grad != "none") NLL-FD Hessian (grad = "none"). Default eps^(1/5) (~2.5e-3) larger textbook eps^(1/4) account MC noise NLL gradient evaluations; empirically matches analytical (sensitivity-equation) Hessian ground truth. Increase (e.g. 5e-3 1e-2) Hessian non-positive definite. phases Numeric vector box-constraint half-widths, one per phase. Phases progressively tighten search region. convcrit Convergence criterion: phase ends |approx - exact| < convcrit. max_worse Stop phase many consecutive worsening iterations. covMethod Covariance method: "r" (numerical Hessian) "none". cov_n_sim Number MC samples covariance (Hessian) step. samples reduce MC noise NLL evaluations. NLL-based Hessian (grad = "none") uses central second difference NLL Sobol sequence (CRN) every perturbed point, noise largely cancels cov_n_sim = 10000 (default) sufficient models. n_restarts Number optimization restarts. Runs parallel workers > 1. restart_sd Standard deviation structural theta perturbations restart initialisation. workers Number parallel workers multi-restart. 1 (default) runs restarts sequentially. Values > 1 use PSOCK cluster Windows fork workers Unix/macOS. Workers stopped automatically restart phase cores available Hessian step. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation; passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery . returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object (useful debugging). ... Additional arguments (none allowed; triggers error).

Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. title: admixr2 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Monte Carlo estimator (est = "admc") via admControl(). Iterative Reweighting Monte Carlo estimator (est = "adirmc") via adirmcControl(). Analytical CRN gradient sensitivity equations (grad = "sens"). Multi-restart parallelism via furrr/future. Diagnostic plots: observed vs predicted mean/covariance, NLL trace, parameter trace. Integrates nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem.
